arts.ttl: Leeds reach verbal agreement to sign PL winner for next season
arts.sum: Leeds United are reportedly looking to bring back former midfielder Kalvin Phillips, who is now edging closer to a sensational return to Elland Road. Despite a recent dip in form that has put their automatic promotion hopes in jeopardy, Leeds have been making moves behind the scenes to bring Phillips back home. The club has […] The post Leeds reach verbal agreement to sign PL winner for next season appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Leeds United team news vs Millwall as Daniel Farke makes one change
arts.sum: Joe Rothwell starts for Leeds United in tonight's game against Millwall with Daniel Farke placing Ilia Gruev on the bench. Rothwell was benched for the defeat to Portsmouth on Sunday in place of Gruev, but Farke goes with the same line up that drew against West Brom 11 days ago.

arts.ttl: 49ers sent clear Leeds United transfer demand before summer plan is unveiled amid title aims
arts.sum: Over 1,600 people took part in the survey, with 83 per cent calling on United to permanently sign Rothwell . Rothwell and Solomon are due to return to their parent clubs Bournemouth and Tottenham Hotspur respectively following the climax of the 2024/25 season.

arts.ttl: Aaronson out of USMNT squad, Poch explains snub
arts.sum: However, there won’t be any international duty for Brenden Aaronson, left out of Mauricio Pochettino’s USMNT squad: Leeds United are looking to go into the upcoming international break top of the league with a decent cushion, starting tonight against Millwall.

arts.ttl: Is Leeds United vs Millwall on TV tonight? Channel, live stream details, kick-off time
arts.sum: With Sky Sports+ broadcasting Leeds vs Millwall, that match will also be available to live stream on Wednesday night. Leeds United return to Championship action this evening when they take on Millwall at Elland Road.

arts.ttl: Chris Wilder’s bleak Sheffield United assessment should be music to Leeds United fans’ ears
arts.sum: For Leeds, they looked to Bristol City, West Brom and Preston to secure positive results away at Sheffield United, Burnley and Sunderland, and they delivered. Leeds United can move two points clear at the top of the Championship tonight with victory over Millwall, following draws for Sheffield United and others on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Graeme Souness claims Leeds United could become Rangers' 'feeder club' if 49ers takeover happens
arts.sum: Graeme Souness reckons Leeds United may very well find themselves in the role of a feeder club for Rangers if a 49ers-inclusive consortium takes over the Scottish Premiership giants. Marathe, who dominates as the president of 49ers Enterprises and the chairman at Leeds, could usher in an era where Daniel Farke's squad operates under the Gers' wing, suggests ex-Rangers icon Souness.

arts.ttl: Kalvin Phillips' Leeds United return update as Whites discover Man City transfer asking price
arts.sum: Leeds United will have to stump up around £20million if they want to bring ex-midfielder Kalvin Phillips back to Elland Road this summer, it has been claimed. While David Moyes handed Phillips a chance to shine there, he failed to impress, making a couple of high-profile mistakes which saw him drop down the pecking order as he ended up playing just 15 minutes in West Ham's final eight league matches and returned to City in the summer.

arts.ttl: Thierry Henry rates Raphinha's Ballon d'Or chances in fresh verdict on ex-Leeds United star
arts.sum: Thierry Henry reckons that Raphinha is the leading candidate to win the Ballon d'Or this year after impressing for Barcelona in the Champions League this season. "Raphinha is on a lot of goals with just one penalty - I'm not saying Kane shouldn't take penalties - and Mohamed Salah is having a season in the Premier League that we will never see again.

arts.ttl: Leeds’ rivals Sunderland played against 12-men Preston
arts.sum: All of the top four had home fixtures this weekend with Leeds playing tonight against Millwall, having to respond to whatever Sheffield United, Burnley, and Sunderland did on Tuesday. Leeds United were handed a boost last night when all three of the sides directly behind them failed to win at home, including Sunderland needing a late equaliser at home to Preston.
